title: Average Calculator — Mean, Median, Mode & More
description: Calculate mean, median, mode, range, and standard deviation from any list of numbers. Instant statistics for students and professionals.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: What is the difference between mean and median?
faq.a1: Mean is the arithmetic average, sensitive to outliers. Median is the middle value when data is sorted, robust to extreme values. For example, in {1,2,3,100}, mean = 26.5, median = 2.5.
faq.q2: How is mode calculated if no number repeats?
faq.a2: If no number appears more than once, the dataset has no mode. Some calculators may report 'no mode' or list all values. Our calculator returns the most frequent value(s); if all are unique, mode is undefined.
faq.q3: When should I use median instead of mean?
faq.a3: Use median for skewed distributions or when outliers are present, such as income data. Mean is appropriate for symmetric distributions without extreme values. Median better represents a typical value in skewed data.
faq.q4: What does the range tell me about my data?
faq.a4: Range = maximum - minimum. It measures the total spread of the data. A large range indicates high variability, while a small range suggests data points are close together. However, range is sensitive to outliers.
faq.q5: Is there a standard reference for calculating mean and median?
faq.a5: Yes, the formulas follow standard statistical definitions as per ISO 3534-1 and the American Statistical Association. Mean and median are fundamental descriptive statistics used universally in data analysis.

content.whatIs: The average calculator computes the mean, median, mode, range, count, and sum of a list of numbers. The mean is the arithmetic average, calculated by summing all numbers and dividing by the count. It provides a quick snapshot of central tendency and spread.
content.formula: Mean = (sum of all numbers) / (count of numbers). Median is the middle value when numbers are sorted; if even count, median = average of two middle numbers. Mode is the most frequent value(s). Range = maximum - minimum. Standard deviation (if included) measures dispersion.
content.example: For numbers 2, 4, 4, 6, 8: sum = 24, count = 5, mean = 24/5 = 4.8. Sorted: 2,4,4,6,8, median = 4 (third value). Mode = 4 (appears twice). Range = 8-2 = 6.
content.howItWorks_1: Enter your list of numbers in the input field, separated by commas or spaces. Each number can be positive, negative, or decimal.
content.howItWorks_2: Click the Calculate button. The tool processes your numbers: it counts them, sums them, sorts them for median and mode, and computes range and standard deviation.
content.howItWorks_3: Review the results: Mean (average), Median (middle value), Mode (most frequent), Range (spread), Count (number of entries), and Sum (total). Use these to understand your data's central tendency and variability.
